macro_segments.title: Bavli on Daf 35: Capital Cases Must Finish by Day; Sleep-on-Judgment Rule

micro_segments.title: Capital Cases Judged and Finished Only in Daytime (From Verse ve'Hoka Otam)
Rav Shimi bar Chiyya: Execution Must Happen 'Neged Ha'Shemesh' (Before Sunset)
Rav Chisda: 'Hoka'ah' (Hanging) as the Form of Execution—Givonim Story
Interpretation of Verse: Moshe Commands to Judge, Not to Execute Leaders
Why Multiple Courts: Not to Judge Two Separate Deaths on Same Day

macro_segments.title: Why Not Begin on Friday and Conclude on Sunday: Fear of Forgetting Arguments

micro_segments.title: Question: Why Not Judge Friday, Sleep Shabbat, Finish Sunday?
If Guilty Verdict Found, Must Sleep Before Execution (Halnat Din)
Cannot Finish on Shabbat (Execution Prohibited on Shabbat)
Cannot Finish on Friday and Execute Sunday (Inuy HaDin—Anguishing the Sentence)
Fear of Forgetting Arguments: Heart of People Forgets What Mind Considered
Conclusion: Cannot Start Erev Shabbat—No Way to Finish Without Break

macro_segments.title: Reish Lakish's Challenge: Kal Vachomer Why Not Execute on Shabbat?

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish Proposes: Met Mitzvah Overrides Avodah, So Why Not Shabbat?
Structure of Kal Vachomer: Avodah > Shabbat, Met Mitzvah > Avodah

Detailed Drasha from Vachoso: Each Family Member Explicit to Exclude Met Mitzvah
If Nazir Were Shechting Pesach/Brit, Still Must Become Tamei for Met Mitzvah

macro_segments.title: Refutation: Verse Prohibits Execution on Shabbat Despite Kal Vachomer

micro_segments.title: Execution Overrides Avodah, But Does Not Override Shabbat—Proof of Limits

macro_segments.title: Reish Lakish's Challenge: Kal Vachomer Why Not Execute on Shabbat? (continued)

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish Escalates: Apply Same Kal Vachomer to Execution—Why Not Execute on Shabbat?

macro_segments.title: Refutation: Verse Prohibits Execution on Shabbat Despite Kal Vachomer (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan: Ritzicha (Execution) Refutes the Kal Vachomer
Rava: Tana of Rabbi Yishmael Rules from Verse 'Lo Tiv'aru Eish' (Do Not Burn Fire)
Verse Clarifies: Not About Melacha Categorization, But Beit Din Context
Why 'Moshvoteichem' Mentioned: To Exclude Execution on Shabbat via Gezeirah Shava
Gezeirah Shava: 'Moshvot' from Fire Verse = 'Moshvot' from Murder Law Verse
Explicit Prohibition: Cannot Execute on Shabbat Even If Execution Overrides Avodah

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Challenge: Kal Vachomer—If Execution Doesn't Override Shabbat, Shouldn't Override Avodah

micro_segments.title: Abaye Inverts: Shabbat > Execution, Avodah > Shabbat, So Avodah > Execution
Therefore Execution Should Not Interfere with Avodah—Contradicts Verse
Structure: Yom Tov > Private Korban, Yom Tov > Execution, So Korban > Execution
Proposed Answer: Verse Speaks Only of Private Korban (Weak, Doesn't Override Shabbat)
Rava Counters: Even by Private Korban, Kal Vachomer Prevents Execution from Interfering
Rava: Even If Private Korban Weak on Yom Tov, Still Verse Must Cover All Korbonot
Final Answer: 'Mizbechi' Means Designated Altar = Tamid (Public Service)
Execution Interferes Even with Public Korban—Ultimate Resolution of Paradox
